title: The History of Chess
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Chess is (0) one of the oldest and most popular board games in the world, played by millions of people across different cultures and age groups. This strategic game has fascinated players for over 1,500 years.||The origins of chess can be traced back (9) to ancient India, where a game called Chaturanga was played in the 6th century. From India, the game spread (10) to Persia and then to the Islamic world. (11) When the Moors invaded Spain in the 8th century, they brought chess with them, and it gradually became popular throughout Europe.||The rules of chess (12) have evolved significantly over the centuries. The modern version of the game, (13) which we play today, was standardised in the 19th century. Chess requires players to think several moves ahead and consider multiple strategies, (14) making it an excellent tool for developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills. The game has (15) also been studied extensively by psychologists and computer scientists interested in artificial intelligence.||Today, chess is played both (16) as a recreational activity and as a competitive sport, with international tournaments attracting the world's best players and offering substantial prize money.

example.text: Chess is (0) of the oldest and most popular board games in the world.

questions.explanation: Expression 'traced back to' means to trace the origin to a place.
Verb 'spread to' indicates expansion towards a place.
Temporal conjunction that introduces the moment of the invasion.
Present perfect: 'have evolved' indicates changes over time.
Relative pronoun referring to the modern version of the game.
Gerundio que introduce una consecuencia o resultado.
Adverb 'also' adds information about another aspect of chess.
Preposition 'as' indicates the role or function: 'played as an activity'.
